english: (like nṓ-bo (ngo bo)); mṓ-ko (mo kho), gźi-kṓ (gzhi kho), rgyú-kṓ (rgyu kho) are said to be used in C. for: the essential, the most important part of a thing, the main point, and the noun substantive may possibly have changed into a substantive pronoun, in a similar manner, as ṅa (nga), I, is connected with nṓ-bo (ngo bo); cf. also kṓ-na (kho na), kṓ-bo (kho bo).
c. termin. to despair of Mil.; to resign, to acquiesce in, to reconcile one's self to; also sems kṓ-tág gcṓd pa (sems kho thag gcod pa) Pth.

adj. and adv. 1. just, exactly, the very, rgyál-pos d̦od-pa kṓ-na yin (rgyal pos 'dod pa kho na yin) that is just what has been wished for by the king Dsl. ☞ 17. sṅá-ma kṓ-na bźín-du (snga ma kho na bzhin du) just as before; d̦i kṓ-na yín-par ṅes ('di kho na yin par nges) he is evidently the very same (man) Mil.; srín-bu kṓ-na d̦rá-ba (srin bu kho na 'dra ba) just like a worm Thgy.; tsul de kṓ-nas (tshul de kho nas) by the very same process Tar. 13, 12; de kṓ-na nyíd-du gyur cig (de kho na nyid du gyur cig) just so may it happen! (at the conclusion of a prayer) Glr.; but de-kṓ-na-nyíd (de kho na nyid), as a philosophical term, is also the translation of the Ssk. táttva, essentiality, truth, implying to the Buddhist nothing but vacuity, the Nirvâna Trig. 20. - 2. only, solely, exclusively, skád-cig kṓ-na (skad cig kho na), only for a moment Dzl. ༣༤༣, 12. d̦od kṓ-nas b̦rél na ('dod kho nas brel na), if taken up merely with lust: séms-can kṓ-na bdé-bar d̦ód-ce-na (sems can kho na bde bar 'dod ce na) as he intended only the welfare of beings Thgy.; Tar.

mas., kṓ-mo (kho mo) fem. pers. pron. 1st. person, I, pl. kṓ-bo-cag (kho bo cag) we, indiscriminately as to the rank of persons, B. and C.; mi kṓ-boi rnam-śés (mi kho bo'i rnam shes) the soul of me the man, i.e. my human soul Mil.; also pleon. kṓ-bo ṅa (kho bo nga).
the Tibetan name for Khatmandu, the capital of Nepaul Glr., Mil.; sometimes also called klui po-bráṅ (klu'i pho brang), prob. on account of the mineral treasures supposed to abound in that country.
perh. misprinted for kom (khom) knapsack, wallet Mil., or else a secondary form of that word.

occurs only in *kṓ-yú skór-ce (*kho yu skor ce) (perh. col. for k̦or-yyúl ('khor g.yul)) W. to thrash, which is done by driving a number of oxen fastened together round a pole that stands in the middle of the thrashing-floor.

Cs. also kór-sa (khor sa), circumference; circumjacent space; also fence, surrounding wall; kṓ-ra k̦or-yúg-tu (kho ra 'khor yug tu), (k̦or-) k̦or-yúg-tu (('khor) 'khor yug tu), in a circle, in circumference, frq. in measuring; also round about, all round, e.g. to flow, to encompass; k̦or-yúg kùn-tu ('khor yug kun tu) in the whole circuit, round about.

1. Cs.: bigness, robustness (Lex. पीवरबाहु), kṓ-lág-yáṅs-pa (kho lag yangs pa) big, prominent limbs; Sch.: kṓ-lág cḗ-ba (kho lag che ba) a large space. - 2. Lh.; dumpling, made of rtsám-pa (rtsam pa) and beer; Ld.: pap of rtsám-pa (rtsam pa) and tea, called spags (spags) in C.
1. frq. for koṅ(-pa) (khong (pa)), the interior, inside; v. also kóg-pa (khog pa) and kóg-ma (khog ma). - 2. for kogs (khogs), kogs (khogs) q.v. - 3. for g̦égs-pa ('gegs pa).

sometimes kóg-ma (khog ma), kog (khog), the trunk of the body, śa-kóg (sha khog) the body of an animal cut up for food; *śa-kóg dál-ce (*sha khog dal ce), śig-ce* (*shig ce) to cut up a carcass; *kóg-tu (*khog tu), kog náṅ-du* (khog nang du) within the body.
C. pot, earthen vessel = pru (phru); kóg-cén (khog chen) large pot.
cough Med., kógs-pa (khogs pa) to cough.

rarely kṓṅ-pa (khong pa), pers. pron. 3d. person, he, she; like kṓ (kho) it is of far less frequency in the earlier literature than in the later; at present it is in W. used as the respectful word for he, but in C., acc. to Lewin, as plur., = they; koṅ-gi (khong gi) his, her; pl. koṅ-rnams (khong rnams), koṅ-cag (khong cag), koṅ-tso koṅ-cag-rnams (khong tsho khong cag rnams); koṅ-ráṅ (khong rang) and koṅ-nyíd (khong nyid) he himself; rgyál-po koṅ-ráṅ yin dgóṅs-nas (rgyal po khong rang yin dgongs nas) the king supposing that he himself was meant Glr.
1. prov. for koṅ-pa (khong pa). - 2. the inside, inward parts, prov. kóg-pa (khog pa) (Cs. also: the veins); koṅ-du (khong du), koṅ-na (khong na), koṅ-nas (khong nas) adv. and postp. in, within, from
